strategic_profile.executive_summary: Auburn University is a public land-, sea-, and space-grant research university known for its robust engineering, business, and agriculture programs. It offers a quintessential Southern college experience characterized by intense school spirit, SEC athletics, and a tight-knit community known as the 'Auburn Family.' In recent years, Auburn has experienced a massive surge in applications, drastically reducing its acceptance rate and transforming it into a highly selective institution.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic but increasingly numbers-focused (phasing out test-optional)

analyst_takeaways.insight: Applications have surged from roughly 18,000 in 2020 to over 55,000 for Fall 2024, driving the acceptance rate down from 85% to approximately 46%.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Auburn can no longer be treated as a safe backup school; out-of-state and average-stat applicants face fierce competition.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Auburn is officially ending its test-optional pilot program, becoming test-preferred for Fall 2026 (requiring 3.6+ GPA to even apply without a test) and completely test-required by Fall 2027.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must prioritize standardized testing, as applying test-optional severely limits admission and scholarship odds.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus revolves around SEC football, heavy Greek life participation (~30%), and deep-rooted traditions.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking a traditional, spirited, and conservative-leaning Southern university will find a strong fit here.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and traditional Southern campus culture heavily influenced by SEC sports (Jordan-Hare Stadium) and Greek life. Students frequently highlight the strong, welcoming community ('Auburn Family') and high overall happiness, though some reviews note a lack of racial and socioeconomic diversity.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Tiger Transit provides comprehensive bus service for students living on and off campus, which is vital as on-campus parking is notoriously difficult and freshman housing is not guaranteed.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 institution with Very High Research Activity. Auburn spends over $147M in Life Sciences and $108M in Engineering research annually, offering robust undergraduate research opportunities.

current_status.test_policy_details: Auburn is phasing out test-optional admissions. For Fall 2026, less than 5% of applicants (who must have a 3.6+ GPA) may be admitted without tests. Beginning Fall 2027, ACT or SAT scores are strictly required for all first-year applicants.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data is not publicly disclosed by Auburn University. The yield rate indicates moderate enrollment melt to cross-admit Southern flagships.
Waitlist data not publicly disclosed. Marks the beginning of application surge after joining Common App.

gpa.notes: Average GPA for test-optional admits was 4.2.

testing.policy_note: Auburn superscores both the ACT and SAT. Test submission rate is expected to reach 100% by 2027.

colleges.name: Harbert College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking strong placement in the Southeast with top firms (Deloitte, PwC). The Supply Chain Management program is highly ranked nationally.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard campus housing; no distinct business dorms.
colleges.student_archetype: Career-oriented, network-driven students heavily involved in Greek life and campus organizations.

core_courses: ISMN 2140 Introduction To Management Information Systems
SCMN 2150 Ops: Mngt Of Business Processes

colleges.name: Samuel Ginn College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for strong STEM students. One of the top engineering programs in the Southeast, boasting a very high post-graduation employment rate.

core_courses: COMP 3350 Computer Organization and Assembly Language Programming

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.notes: Ranked among the highest paying majors out of Auburn.

colleges.name: College of Architecture, Design and Construction
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly competitive, capped majors for creative students. Excellent for Architecture and Building Science.

majors.name: Architecture
majors.special_requirements: Capped major; highly competitive internal transfer if not admitted directly.
majors.notes: Very limited seats for transfer/change of major students.

application_deadlines.notes: Early Action Round 1. Decisions released mid-October.
Early Action Round 2. Decisions released mid-November.
Early Action Round 3. Decisions released mid-December.
Early Action Round 4. Decisions released early February.
Regular Decision. Decisions released early March.

supplemental_requirements.details: Auburn completely removed supplemental essay requirements to simplify the application process, relying strictly on grades, rigor, and test scores.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Auburn evaluates heavily on quantitative metrics rather than soft qualitative factors, making GPA, transcript rigor, and ACT/SAT superscores the driving forces behind admission.

major_selection_tactics: Auburn generally admits into the university rather than specific majors, so applying to an 'easier' major does not provide a backdoor for general admission.
Apply during Early Action. Auburn fills a vast majority of its class and allocates almost all merit scholarships to students who apply by the December 1st EA priority deadline.
Submit test scores. Applying test-optional currently puts applicants at a significant disadvantage for admission and renders them largely ineligible for merit scholarships.
college_ranking_tactics: Ensure you have a 3.8+ Unweighted GPA and 28+ ACT to be highly competitive, especially if applying from out-of-state.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Auburn allows the selection of an alternate major. However, since the university evaluates general admission first, the alternate major primarily matters if your first choice is one of the few capped programs (like Architecture or Nursing) and you are not selected for it.

financials.tuition_model: Per credit hour for part-time, flat rate for full-time (12+ hours).

scholarships.name: National Scholars Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition for National Merit Finalists, plus potential stipends.

scholarships.name: Access and Affordability Scholarships
scholarships.benefits: Helps cover tuition for Alabama residents with significant financial need; priority to first-generation students.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 4 or 5 generally yield full course credit.
exceptions: A score of 3 on some exams (like Foreign Languages) may yield lower-division ungraded hours but not full sequence credit.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to bypass core requirements, but does not fulfill residency requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Credit awarded for Higher Level exams.

tools: Alabama Statewide Articulation and General Studies agreement (STARS)
transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum transfer credit allowed is one-half of the student's curriculum (not exceeding 64 semester hours). 25% of total hours and 50% of major courses must be taken in residence.

what_it_takes: Applying early in the cycle (Early Action)
common_activities: Auburn Football Tailgating and 'Rolling Toomer's Corner'
essay_tips: Auburn currently does not require supplemental essays, making transcript optimization and test prep the primary focus.
red_flags: Misconduct disclosures (academic or criminal) on the application trigger a thorough review by the Disclosure Review Committee and can lead to denial.
Dropping rigor in senior year. Auburn heavily reviews planned 12th-grade courses.
insights: Freshman housing is not guaranteed and fills extremely fast. Students must pay their enrollment deposit immediately upon acceptance to secure a spot on the housing list.
Auburn has removed its holistic essay review, making the admissions process almost entirely numbers-driven.
The 'Auburn Family' is a real cultural phenomenon; students are highly loyal and school spirit is pervasive.
